zh.lifeSummary: 當Hywel Hen ap Cadwgon ap Trahaearn於1245年出生在英國威爾斯布雷康史爾時，他的父親Hywel ap Trahaearn76歲，母親Nest verch Gruffudd of Dynevor69歲。他和Mrs. Hywel Hen ap Cadwgan至少有1個兒子。他去世在他的家鄉。

nameMeanings.name: Cadwgon
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: Welsh:  see  Cadogan  .

variants.name: Cadogan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Dermot.

Welsh (Pembrokeshire and Glamorgan) and Irish (of Welsh origin): from the Old Welsh personal name Catgucaun, Catguocawn, modernized as Cadwgon. It is composed of cad ‘battle’ + gogawn ‘honour, glory’. See also Wogan . The surname is stressed, like its Welsh original, on the penultimate syllable, hence its occasional development to Duggan .
Irish: from Ó Ceadagáin ‘descendant of Ceadagán’.

nameMeanings.name: ap
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Dutch: occupational name for a cobbler.
Slovenian: nickname for a talkative person, from lapati ‘to babble’, or a topographic name from dialect lap ‘abyss’. Compare Lapp 4.

variants.name: Lapp
meanings: German, English, and Dutch: from Middle High German and Middle English lappe, Middle Dutch lap ‘cloth, patch, rag’, a metonymic occupational name for a mender of clothes or shoes, or a nickname given to someone who wore ragged clothes or garments with loose folds, flaps, or hanging sleeves, or who had fleshy folds of skin on his face, neck, or body.

German: nickname for a simple-minded person, from Middle Low German lappe ‘fool’. This surname (in any of the two possible senses; see also 1 above) is also found in France (mainly Alsace, also Lorraine).
English: nickname from Anglo-Norman French lappe ‘burdock’, perhaps for someone with a prickly character.

nameMeanings.name: Trahaearn
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: Welsh:  see  Treharne  .

variants.name: Treharne
meanings: Welsh (Glamorgan and Carmarthenshire): from the Welsh personal name Trahaearn, composed of the elements tra ‘most, very’ + haearn ‘iron’.

nameMeanings.name: Hywel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Traditional: originally a byname from a vocabulary word meaning ‘eminent, conspicuous’; it was common in the Middle Ages and was revived in the 20th-century.

variants.name: Hywell
meanings: Traditional: originally a byname from a vocabulary word meaning ‘eminent, conspicuous’; it was common in the Middle Ages and was revived in the 20th-century.

variants.name: Howell
meanings: Anglicized form of the Welsh name Hywel , or a transferred use of the surname derived from that name.

nameMeanings.name: Hen
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: 1  ‘Cautious’.
2  ‘Deep’.

nameMeanings.name: ap
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of any of the names beginning with this syllable.

variants.name: Alexander
meanings: From the Latin form of the Greek name Alexandros, from alexein ‘to defend’ + anēr ‘man, warrior’ (genitive andros). The name became extremely popular in the post-classical period, and was borne by several individuals in the New Testament and some early Christian saints. Its use as a common given name throughout Europe, however, derives largely from the fame of Alexander the Great , King of Macedon ( 356–323 bc ), around whom a large body of popular legend grew up in late antiquity, much of which came to be embodied in the medieval ‘Alexander romances’.

variants.name: Alan
meanings: Of Celtic origin and uncertain derivation (possibly a diminutive of a word meaning ‘rock’). It was introduced into England by Breton followers of William the Conqueror , most notably Alan , Earl of Brittany, who was rewarded for his services with vast estates in the newly conquered kingdom. In Britain the variants Allan and Allen are considerably less frequent, and generally represent transferred uses of surname forms, whereas in America all three forms of the name are approximately equally common. See also Alun .

variants.name: Albert
meanings: From an Old French name, Albert, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from adal ‘noble’ + berht ‘bright, famous’. This was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to England, displacing the Old English form Æþelbeorht. The name is popular in a variety of forms in Western Europe, and has been traditional in a number of European princely families. It was out of favour in England for centuries, however, and the revival of its popularity in the 19th century was largely in honour of Queen Victoria's consort, Prince Albert of Saxe-Coburg-Gotha .

en.lifeSummary: When Hywel Hen ap Cadwgon ap Trahaearn was born in 1245, in Breconshire, Wales, United Kingdom, his father, Hywel ap Trahaearn, was 76 and his mother, Nest verch Gruffudd of Dynevor, was 69. He had at least 1 son with Mrs. Hywel Hen ap Cadwgan. He died in his hometown.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Hywel Hen ap Cadwgon ap Trahaearn est né en 1245, à Breconshire, Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni, son père, Hywel ap Trahaearn, avait 76 ans et sa mère, Nest verch Gruffudd of Dynevor, avait 69 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fils avec Mrs. Hywel Hen ap Cadwgan. Il est décédé dans sa ville natale.

de.lifeSummary: Als Hywel Hen ap Cadwgon ap Trahaearn 1245 in Breconshire, Wales, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Hywel ap Trahaearn 76 und seine Mutter Nest verch Gruffudd of Dynevor 69. Er hatte mit Mrs. Hywel Hen ap Cadwgan mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Hywel Hen ap Cadwgon ap Trahaearn è nato il nel 1245 nel Breconshire, Galles, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Hywel ap Trahaearn, aveva 76 anni e il suo madre, Nest verch Gruffudd of Dynevor, aveva 69 anni. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio con Mrs. Hywel Hen ap Cadwgan. Morì nel sua cittadina.

ja.lifeSummary: Hywel Hen ap Cadwgon ap Trahaearnが1245年にイギリスウェールズブレコンシュアイアで生まれた時，父Hywel ap Trahaearnは76歳，母Nest verch Gruffudd of Dynevorは69歳であった。彼はMrs. Hywel Hen ap Cadwganとの間に少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼は故郷で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Hywel Hen ap Cadwgon ap Trahaearn(이)가 1245년 영국 웨일스 브레콘실르에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Hywel ap Trahaearn(은)는 76세였고, 그의 어머니 Nest verch Gruffudd of Dynevor(은)는 69세였다. 그는 Mrs. Hywel Hen ap Cadwgan(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 그의 고향에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Hywel Hen ap Cadwgon ap Trahaearn nasceu em 1245, em Breconshire, Gales, United Kingdom, seu pai, Hywel ap Trahaearn, tinha 76 anos e sua mãe, Nest verch Gruffudd of Dynevor, tinha 69 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho com Mrs. Hywel Hen ap Cadwgan. Ele faleceu em sua cidade natal.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Hywel Hen ap Cadwgon ap Trahaearn родился в 1245 г. в местности брекуншир, Уэльс, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Hywel ap Trahaearn было 76 лет, а матери, которую звали Nest verch Gruffudd of Dynevor, 69. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын от Mrs. Hywel Hen ap Cadwgan. Он умер в его родном городе.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Hywel Hen ap Cadwgon ap Trahaearn nació en 1245, en Breconshire, Gales, Reino Unido, su papá, Hywel ap Trahaearn, tenía 76 años y su mamá, Nest verch Gruffudd of Dynevor, tenía 69 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo con Mrs. Hywel Hen ap Cadwgan. Murió en su pueblo.
